---
title: "4b. Unique Proteins vs Cardiovascular Health"
author: "Avril Metcalfe-Roach"
output: html_document
---

```{r LOAD_DATA}
library(tidyverse)

# Change as needed - I can't upload the data to Github for privacy reasons
my_data_location = "~/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UBC/CMMI/ANALYSES/Pilot/Data/"

# Load the annotated genes
annot = readxl::read_xlsx('../Outputs/3. MGX Unique UniRef50IDs - Annotated.xlsx',sheet='No unknown or deleted')

# Load the gene counts
gene = data.table::fread(paste0(my_data_location,'humann_exports_mgx_g4h/mgx_genefamilies_unstratified.tsv')) %>% 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ . / sum(.))) %>%
  mutate(`# Gene Family` = sapply(.$`# Gene Family`, function(x) str_split(x,'[:]')[[1]][1])) %>% 
  mutate(`# Gene Family` = str_replace_all(.$`# Gene Family`, 'UniRef50_', ''))
```

Only considering proteins that are present in at least 3 people.

```{r FILTER_AND_ANNOTATE}
# Filter and annotate
gene_filt = gene %>% 
  filter(`# Gene Family` %in% c(annot$From)) %>% 
  rename(feature = `# Gene Family`) 

gene_prev = gene_filt %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = -feature, names_to = 'sample', values_to = 'abundance') %>% 
  group_by(feature) %>% 
  summarise(prev = sum(abundance > 0)) %>%  # Number of people with R. lac
  filter(prev>3) # Present in more than 3 people
```


```{r ADD_METADATA}
# Load phyloseq objct
ps = readRDS(paste0(my_data_location,'pilot_metagenomic_phyloseq_g4h.rds'))
sam = data.frame(ps@sam_data) %>% 
  rownames_to_column('Sample')

# Fix non-real values in table
sam[sam==-7] = NA # -7 is NA
sam[sam==9] = NA # 9 = 'Don't know'

# Fix names, add metadata ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
# sample key
key <- read_csv(paste0(my_data_location,"sample_key.csv"))

gene_annot = gene_filt %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -feature, names_to = 'Sample', values_to = 'abundance') %>%
  mutate(Sample = sapply(.$Sample,function(x) str_split(x, pattern = "_",)[[1]][1] %>% as.numeric())) %>% 
   left_join(key %>% rename(Sample=old)) %>%
  select(-Sample) %>%
  rename(Sample=new) %>% 
  left_join(sam)
```

gene_annot has one row per sample/UniRef50 ID pair.

```{r EXPLORE_CV_DATA}
# Extract all the ROME constipation questions
cardio = c(
  'CMMI_DIS_CARDIO_EVER',
  'CMMI_DIS_HBP_EVER',
  'CMMI_DIS_HIGH_CHOL_EVER',
  'CMMI_DIS_MI_EVER',
  'CMMI_DIS_HF_EVER',
  'CMMI_DIS_CHD_EVER',
  'CMMI_DIS_ATRIAL_EVER',
  'CMMI_DIS_ARRHYTHMIA_EVER',
  'CMMI_DIS_ANGINA_EVER',
  'CMMI_DIS_HM_EVER',
  'CMMI_DIS_VHD_EVER',
  'CMMI_DIS_THROMBO_EVER',
  'CMMI_DIS_HEMO_EVER'
)

temp = sam %>% select(Sample,all_of(cardio)) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(CMMI_DIS_CARDIO_EVER)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Sample, names_to = 'variable', values_to = 'value') %>%
  group_by(variable, value) %>%
  summarise(n = n()) %>%
  ungroup() %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = value, values_from = n, values_fill = 0)

# Add descriptions
desc = readxl::read_xlsx(paste0(my_data_location,'CMMI_Healthy Ager Pilot Data_ALL VARIABLES_Sep 18 2025.xlsx'), sheet = 'Data Dictionary') %>% 
  select(variable_name, description) %>% 
  rename(variable = variable_name) %>% 
  filter(variable %in% temp$variable) %>% 
  right_join(temp) %>%
  select(variable, description, everything())

# Inspection of desc: only a few variables have enough variance to assess. Keeping anything with at least 4 non-zero values.
to_test = temp %>% filter(`1` > 1) %>% pull(variable)
to_test
```

```{r CV_STATS}
# Rationale for Wilcoxon test: every question is heavily skewed toward no issues, so it has to be nonparametric. All are binary.
final_data = gene_annot %>% 
  select(feature, abundance, Sample, all_of(to_test)) %>%
  filter(feature %in% gene_prev$feature) %>%
  filter(!is.na(CMMI_DIS_CARDIO_EVER)) %>%
  # Make this variable match the formatting of the others - 0 = no issues, 1 = any issues
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(feature:Sample), names_to = 'variable', values_to = 'value') %>%
  filter(!is.na(value)) %>% 
  mutate(value = ifelse(value == 0, 'No', 'Yes')) # Make this binary for plotting purposes

stats = final_data %>%
  group_by(feature, variable) %>%
  summarise(cor = wilcox.test(abundance ~ as.character(value))$statistic,
            pval = wilcox.test(abundance ~ as.character(value))$p.value) %>%
  ungroup() %>% 
  filter(!is.nan(cor))

# Plot these results as a heatmap
plot_data = stats %>% left_join(desc) %>% 
  # Not sure why there are NAs - figure out later
  filter(!is.na(feature))

# P VALUES - FILT
plot_wide = plot_data %>% select(variable,feature,pval) %>% 
  filter(pval < 0.05) %>% 
  mutate(pval = -log10(pval)) %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = variable, values_from = pval) %>% 
  column_to_rownames('feature')
plot_wide[is.na(plot_wide)] = 0 # Set NA values to 0 for plotting

nrow(plot_wide) # 29 features
# Generate clustered heatmap with dendrogram on both axes
library(pheatmap)
p = pheatmap(
  plot_wide,
  color = colorRampPalette(c("white", "blue"))(100),
  scale = "none",               # Don't scale the data since -log10(p) is already interpretable
  cluster_cols = T,
  clustering_distance_rows = "euclidean",
  clustering_distance_cols = "euclidean",
  clustering_method = "ward.D2",
  show_rownames = TRUE,
  show_colnames = TRUE,
  filename="../Outputs/4b. Unique Proteins vs Cardiovascular Function - Heatmap.png"
)
```
